method_atom: constructing reducible (non-irreducible) representations of an operator algebra defined by canonical commutation and anticommutation relations, so that the generators are realized as genuine well-defined operators rather than distribution-valued objects, with the reducibility acting as a regularization that can yield finite rather than divergent results.
object_atom: the second quantization of relativistic Dirac (fermionic) and Maxwell (bosonic/electromagnetic) fields in quantum field theory.
brief_reason: the transferable machinery is the use of reducible representations of a canonical (anti)commutation algebra to obtain honest operators and finite outcomes; the domain-specific object is the quantized Dirac and Maxwell fields it is applied to.
